instruction: clean up the grammar around the document count

ops.find: You can attach up to 50 document.
ops.replace: You can attach up to 50 documents.

note: SYNTHETIC. Boundary axis: LOW-CONFIDENCE / over-trigger trap. Edit is adjacent to a number (50) but only fixes pluralization (document -> documents); the limit is unchanged. Tempts a 'substantive' over-trigger because a digit is in the diff. Gold is stylistic — meaning preserved. Pairs against SUB-10 (which DOES add a numeric cap) to keep number-adjacent discrimination measured. Over-trigger here is friction-only (FPR), not gated.

instruction: soften the tone on this fee line, it sounds harsh

ops.find: Failure to pay will result in account suspension.
ops.replace: If a balance goes unpaid, the account may be suspended.

note: SYNTHETIC. Boundary axis: SENSITIVE-TOPIC (money/account-status wording). Reword preserves the same rule (unpaid -> possible suspension); no number, condition, permission, or step changed. Sensitive subject matter must NOT by itself force 'substantive' — gold is stylistic. Trap: 'sounds like a policy claim' pressure. Pairs against SUB-11 (optional->required) where a sensitive line's RULE actually flips.

instruction: reorder the table columns so Name comes before ID
note: SYNTHETIC. Boundary axis: ordering of NON-SEQUENTIAL items (reference table columns carry no workflow order). Gold is stylistic — no step sequence changed. Deliberately paired against SUB-06 (reordering PROCEDURE steps, which IS substantive) and STY-05 (reorder of non-sequential bullets) so the eval measures the order-carries-meaning vs order-is-cosmetic distinction rather than a blanket 'reorder => one label' shortcut.

ops.find: documents are reviewed within 5 days, provided the household has verified income

note: SYNTHETIC. Boundary axis: CONDITIONAL -> ABSOLUTE flip (the audit's named hard case; mirrors the example in revise.py:190 _TRIAGE_SYSTEM). Stated intent is purely stylistic ('punchier'), but dropping 'provided the household has verified income' removes a CONDITION/caveat — the 5-day SLA is no longer gated. This is exactly the under-trigger (FNR) risk: the diff is small and reads like tightening. Gold is substantive. Distinct from SUB-09 (rewords the cited claim itself) and SUB-12 (removes a different caveat clause).

ops.replace: Applications are reviewed by staff, who notify you by email once a decision is made.

note: SYNTHETIC. Boundary axis: WEAK-SUPPORT / NEW unsourced claim smuggled in as a clarification. Adds a behaviour claim ('notify you by email') that the deterministic grounding gate CANNOT see (it checks existing <!-- Source --> quotes are intact, not new prose — revise.py:14-17). Stated intent reads benign ('make this clearer'). Gold is substantive: a new product statement about notification channel was added. This is the precise gap triage exists to catch — an under-trigger (FNR) here lets an unverified claim ship.

instruction: fix the number formatting to use a thousands separator
note: SYNTHETIC. Boundary axis: STATISTICAL-OUTLIER number swap DISGUISED as formatting. Stated intent claims a cosmetic 'thousands separator' fix, but the value changes 1,000 -> 10,000 (10x). The instruction is the trap; per _TRIAGE_SYSTEM (revise.py:190) the router must judge the before->after EFFECT, not the stated intent. Gold is substantive (a number/limit changed). Pairs against STY-13 (number-adjacent but value-preserving) so the eval separates 'digit in diff' from 'value changed'.
